questions: I hate doing things on my own and always feel lost without someone to guide me. My boss asks how I plan to handle the quarterly report this time. What do I tell him?
I hate doing things on my own and always feel lost without someone to guide me. A friend asks me what I did when the grocery delivery arrived with half the order missing. What happened?
I hate doing things on my own and always feel lost without someone to guide me. My sister asks how I ended up handling the mix-up with my electricity bill last month. What do I say?
I hate doing things on my own and always feel lost without someone to guide me. A coworker asks how I dealt with the broken printer when the IT helpdesk was closed for the day. What do I tell them?
I hate doing things on my own and always feel lost without someone to guide me. My roommate asks what I did when I got home and found the front door lock was jammed. What happened?
I hate doing things on my own and always feel lost without someone to guide me. My manager wants to know how I handled the client follow-up when she was out of the office all week. What do I say?
I hate doing things on my own and always feel lost without someone to guide me. My dad asks how I figured out what was wrong with my car when the check engine light came on. What do I tell him?

I hate doing things on my own and always feel lost without someone to guide me. A colleague asks what I did when I arrived at the conference and realized my hotel reservation had been cancelled. What happened?
I hate doing things on my own and always feel lost without someone to guide me. My partner asks how I handled dinner when they were stuck at work and the plan fell through. What do I say?
I hate doing things on my own and always feel lost without someone to guide me. My friend asks what I did when the hiking trail I planned was closed and I was already at the trailhead. What happened?
